questions.question: Directive Principles of State Policy are stated in which part of the Constitution?
questions.explanation: Directive Principles of State Policy are stated in Part IV of the Indian Constitution, under Articles 36 to 51.

questions.question: Directive Principles of State Policy are covered under which Articles?
questions.explanation: Directive Principles are stated in Articles 36 to 51 of the Indian Constitution.

questions.question: The Directive Principles of State Policy were inspired by the Constitution of:
questions.explanation: The Directive Principles were inspired by the Directive Principles stated in the Constitution of Ireland and the principles of Gandhian philosophy.

questions.question: Directive Principles of State Policy are:
questions.explanation: Directive Principles are non-justiciable, meaning they are not enforceable by any court of law.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a basic principle of Gandhian thought?
questions.explanation: The three basic principles of Gandhian thought mentioned in the textbook are Swaraj (self-rule), Sarvodaya (welfare for all) and Swavlamban (self-reliance). Swadhinta is not one of them.

questions.question: Under the Directive Principles, free and compulsory education is to be provided to children up to the age of:
questions.explanation: The state should take steps to impart compulsory and free education to children up to the age of 14 years.

questions.question: Which of the following is a feature of a welfare state?
options: A. It favours the rich over the poor
B. It takes steps to abolish illiteracy, poverty and unemployment
C. It only provides education to the elite
D. It does not provide social benefits
questions.correct_answer: B. It takes steps to abolish illiteracy, poverty and unemployment
questions.explanation: A welfare state takes steps to abolish illiteracy, poverty and unemployment, and provides equal opportunities for all citizens.

questions.question: The Community Development Programme was launched in:
questions.explanation: The Community Development Programme was launched in 1952 as part of development programmes to raise the standard of living.

questions.question: The Integrated Rural Development Programme was launched in:
questions.explanation: The Integrated Rural Development Programme was launched in 1978-79 to raise the standard of living in rural areas.

questions.question: The Mahatma Gandhi Rural Employment Guarantee Act was passed in:
questions.explanation: The Mahatma Gandhi Rural Employment Guarantee Act was passed in 2006 to guarantee rural employment and raise the standard of living.

questions.question: Which amendment added the duty to protect and improve the environment as a Directive Principle?
questions.explanation: The 42nd Amendment added a new directive - the duty of the state to protect and improve the environment and to safeguard the forests and wildlife of the country.

questions.question: Which of the following is a Gandhian Directive Principle?
options: B. Establishing a uniform civil code
C. Promoting cottage industries in rural areas
questions.correct_answer: C. Promoting cottage industries in rural areas
questions.explanation: Promoting cottage industries in rural areas is a Gandhian Directive Principle, while the others are General Principles.

questions.question: Under Directive Principles, the state shall promote the interests of which weaker sections?
options: B. Scheduled Castes and Scheduled Tribes
questions.correct_answer: B. Scheduled Castes and Scheduled Tribes
questions.explanation: The state shall promote educational and economic interests of weaker sections, especially Scheduled Castes and Scheduled Tribes.

questions.question: Directive Principles and Fundamental Rights are:
questions.explanation: Directive Principles and Fundamental Rights are not contradictory but complementary. Both aim at the same thing - the welfare of citizens.

questions.question: Fundamental Rights help in strengthening:
questions.explanation: Fundamental Rights help in strengthening political democracy, while Directive Principles establish social and economic democracy.

questions.question: The Zamindari system was abolished as an impact of which principle?
questions.explanation: The abolition of the Zamindari system was one of the impacts of Directive Principles. Surplus land of landlords was distributed among the landless poor.

questions.question: To promote cottage industries (Article 43), the government established all of the following EXCEPT:
questions.explanation: The government established Village Industries Board, All India Handicraft Board, Silk Board and Coir Board. Heavy Industries Board is not among them.

questions.question: A welfare state is based on the principles of:
options: A. Equality of opportunity and equal distribution of wealth
C. Concentration of wealth among the elite
questions.correct_answer: A. Equality of opportunity and equal distribution of wealth
questions.explanation: A welfare state is based on principles of equality of opportunity and equal distribution of wealth.

questions.question: Which of the following is a General Directive Principle?
options: A. Equal pay for equal work
C. Establishing a uniform civil code
D. Free education up to age 14
questions.correct_answer: C. Establishing a uniform civil code
questions.explanation: Establishing a uniform civil code for all citizens is a General Directive Principle.

questions.question: Under Directive Principles, the state shall encourage settlement of international disputes by:
questions.explanation: The state shall encourage settlement of international disputes by arbitration, meaning mutual agreement.

questions.question: Before Independence, India was not a welfare state because:
options: A. Indians did not want welfare
B. The British were not interested in the welfare of the people
C. India was too rich to need welfare
D. The Constitution did not exist yet
questions.correct_answer: B. The British were not interested in the welfare of the people
questions.explanation: Under British rule, India was not a welfare state because the British were not interested in the welfare of the people. Whatever they did was in the interests of the British government.

questions.question: India is described as a:
options: A. Sovereign, socialist, secular, democratic, republic
questions.correct_answer: A. Sovereign, socialist, secular, democratic, republic
questions.explanation: India is a sovereign, socialist, secular, democratic, republic country as stated in the Constitution.

questions.question: Which programme provides mid-day meals to school children?
options: B. Health and nutritional support programmes
questions.correct_answer: B. Health and nutritional support programmes
questions.explanation: The government introduced various programmes for health and nutritional support to women and children, including maternity relief and mid-day meal for school children.

questions.question: Swaraj means:
questions.explanation: Swaraj means self-rule. It is one of the three basic principles of Gandhian thought.

questions.question: The Directive Principles ensure which type of democracy?
questions.explanation: Directive Principles establish social and economic democracy, while Fundamental Rights guarantee political democracy.
